summary: This cluster focuses on the taxonomic revision of the diatom genus *Amphora sensu lato*, integrating molecular phylogenetics with detailed morphological analysis to resolve species boundaries and evolutionary relationships. Researchers established that the subgenus *Halamphora* is phylogenetically distinct, supporting its reclassification as a separate genus, and formally described *Amphora terroris* as a new species. The studies collectively emphasize that morphological traits remain critical for accurate taxonomic revision, even when molecular data suggest reclassification. Geographically, the research spans diverse marine and estuarine environments, reflecting the global distribution of *Amphora* species.
